title: clausena anisum-olens subsp. mollis
intro: Clausena anisum-olens subsp. mollis, also known as the soft Indian plum or soft sweet orange, is a subspecies of the Clausena anisum-olens plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The leaves and fruits of Clausena anisum-olens subsp. mollis have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a natural remedy for various skin conditions. Clausena anisum-olens subsp. mollis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in cooking and as a flavoring agent, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
